Renaissance Early Modern
19th Century Phenomenology Analytic

Glossary About
Falsafa · 9th – 12th c. CE

Islamic Tradition

The Islamic world receives Greek psychology and transforms it in dialogue with revelation, mysticism, and metaphysics. From Avicenna and Averroes to al-Ghazālī, Ibn Arabi, and Mullā Ṣadrā, mind becomes a site where intellect, soul, illumination, and divine reality meet.

c. 872 – 950

al-Fārābī

The “Second Master”: a systematic philosophy of intellect that frames the Islamic reception of Aristotle.

c. 980 – 1037

Avicenna (Ibn Sīnā)

The floating man and the self-aware intellect: one of the great arguments about the soul.

1126 – 1198

Averroes (Ibn Rushd)

The Commentator: a rigorous Aristotelianism that provokes centuries of European debate on the intellect.

1154 – 1191

Suhrawardī

The philosophy of Illumination: light as the metaphysical texture of soul and knowledge.

1058 – 1111

al-Ghazālī

Skepticism, illumination, and the soul between theology and mystical knowledge.

1165 – 1240

Ibn Arabi

Imagination, unveiling, and the soul’s ascent through divine reality.

c. 1571 – 1640

Mullā Ṣadrā

Substantial motion, soul, and consciousness as intensified being.

Digital Minds · A history of thinking about the mind, from antiquity to the present day.
A personal research project by Fabián. · About · Glossary
